data.body: Plan-review iter2 = BLOCK (12 blocking) — the integrator's review is surfacing genuine cross-cutting issues that need careful per-item investigation. PRECISE REMEDIATION NOTES for resume: (1) SHAPE B / .outcome STRIPPING [crux]: transition_close.py:174-181 wraps the caught exception into CommandError(returncode=1) with 'from None', stripping mamba's err.outcome. FIX: read outcome=getattr(exc,'outcome',None) BEFORE the wrap; set returncode=11 when outcome and outcome.retryable (WAIT_AND_RETRY/RETRY_NOW) else 1; put the class-specific message in the CommandError text. (2) SHAPE A write-site: gate_dispatch._degraded_plan_review_verdict (+ code-review equiv) copies coverage['resolution_class']=outcome.resolution_class.value / coverage['diagnostic']=outcome.diagnostic off the caught error's .outcome. (3) EXIT-2 semantics: do NOT redefine exit 2 (usage error generally; review-plan ALREADY uses 2 for INDETERMINATE per arawana) — only peel the retryable subset to NEW exit 11. (4) AC2 vs AC8 conflict: decide ONE — enumerate consumers AND update the Gerrit-bot/CI to recognize 11 (additive-safe in the gap). (5) session-log write: specify the concrete call (append_session_log(sanitized_diagnostic, summary=...) at the degrade site). (6) MCP: check how FastMCP surfaces tool failures today (_mcp_llm.py) — return the structured dict as the tool RESULT, not raise, matching existing patterns. Investigate _mcp_llm.py before asserting. Design otherwise sound; large story, needs a few more iterations + a multi-file impl (3 schemas, 4 CLI sites, sign_manifest guard in signing.py, exit-codes.md + test_exit_codes.py, 4 ADRs, MCP, session-log, docs).
